name: calva-backseat-driver
description: Make Copilot an Interactive Programmer, tools for VS Code Copilot. Can also be run as an MCP server

calva-backseat-driver.enableMcpReplEvalu.markdownDescription: Expose REPL evaluation through the MCP server (`clojure_evaluate_code`, `clojure_load_file`). Enabled by default — set to `false` to disable. The socket server is local to your machine; this setting controls whether evaluation tools are listed and callable.

calva-backseat-driver.autoStartMCPServer.markdownDescription: Automatically start the MCP server when the extension is activated.

calva-backseat-driver.autoRegisterCursor.markdownDescription: In Cursor, automatically register the Backseat Driver MCP server with the IDE Agent (via `vscode.cursor.mcp.registerServer`). Requires a workspace folder. When enabled, the MCP socket server starts silently on activation. Disable if you prefer manual `.cursor/mcp.json` configuration. Takes effect after window reload.

calva-backseat-driver.mcpSocketServerPor.markdownDescription: Port to start the socket server on. Use `0` to let the server choose a high available port.

calva-backseat-driver.editor.fuzzyLineTa.markdownDescription: Number of lines to search above and below when locating a target line by its text content to decide if the edit should be carried out or not. **Trade-offs**: Higher values tolerate line number shifts but may match incorrect lines if similar code exists nearby. Large errors by the agent may indicate it has stale file content, and you may not want it to edit the file if so.

calva-backseat-driver.editor.lineContext.markdownDescription: Number of lines above and below the target line to show the agent in error responses when an edit operation fails. **Trade-offs**: Larger values provide more context for the agent's retry but may consume more tokens. Or it may not, depending on how the agent uses the information (e.g. if the context contains what it needs, it may be able to avoid a full file reread).

calva-backseat-driver.evaluation.maxLeng.markdownDescription: Maximum number of items to display in collections when pretty-printing REPL evaluation results. Use `0` to disable length limiting. **Purpose**: Prevents large collections from overwhelming the agent's context window.

calva-backseat-driver.evaluation.maxDept.markdownDescription: Maximum nesting depth when pretty-printing REPL evaluation results. Deeper structures are replaced with `##`. Use `0` to disable depth limiting. **Purpose**: Prevents deeply nested structures from overwhelming the agent's context window.

calva-backseat-driver.provideBdSkill.markdownDescription: Provide the Backseat Driver skill to agents

calva-backseat-driver.provideEditSkill.markdownDescription: Provide the Clojure structural editing skill to agents

languageModelTools.name: clojure_evaluate_code
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
**Evaluate Clojure Code** using Calva's REPL connection. "Use the REPL" means this tool. In a Joyride context use `joyride_evaluate_code` instead of this tool.

Result may include `otherWhosSinceLast` — other evaluators active since your last call. If evaluation results contain image data, it will be automatically extracted and returned as viewable images.

Use `description` instead of line comments in the code you are evaluating, for explaining the purpose of the evaluation, or to help yourself think.

Indent code properly before evaluation (agents often get maps misaligned).

When a session supports runtimes you can pass `targetRuntimeId` (from `clojure_list_sessions`) to evaluate on a specific runtime.

Always load the `backseat-driver` skill when using this tool.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Evaluate Clojure or ClojureScript code in the connected REPL.

namespace.description: Fully qualified namespace in which to evaluate the code. E.g. if calling functions in a file you are reading, **it is probably the namespace of that file that should be provided**. If it is the first time you use a namespace, start with evaluating its ns-form in the `user` or `cljs.user` namespace (as appropriate).

replSessionKey.description: The Calva REPL session to evaluate the code via. Use the `list_sessions` tool to discover available sessions.

who.description: A short, stable kebab-case slug identifying you as an evaluator. Think about what the session is about and what your role is in it, then sluggify that role and use it for `who`. Reuse the same slug across all evaluations in a session.

description.description: Succinct human-readable note explaining what you are evaluating and why. Shows up in the repl output tool's log, and is also in the human's output view. Use this *instead* of line comments in the code you are evaluating.

maxImages.description: Maximum number of images to return from image data found in the result. Defaults to 10. Images beyond this cap are replaced with <<image-N-capped>> markers in the text but not returned as image content. Avoid settings this above 20, because Copilot has a hard cap of 20 images.

targetRuntimeId.description: Only when the session supports runtimes (like shadow-cljs). Evaluate on a specific runtime (browser tab / Node process) without changing the editor's connected runtime. Get IDs from `clojure_list_sessions`.

languageModelTools.name: clojure_list_sessions
languageModelTools.modelDescription: **List REPL Sessions** returns information about all available REPL sessions. Use this tool to discover which session keys are available for evaluation. Each session includes the session key, project root, and file glob patterns it handles. The session serving the user's active file is marked with `currentRoutedTarget: true`. If the repl session supports runtimes, the tool will return brief information about this.
languageModelTools.userDescription: List available REPL sessions and their details.

includeAllRuntimes.description: Only valid when the session supports runtimes. List every connected runtime per build. (Without this option you'll get the last active runtime per build plus a count on total number of runtimes.)

languageModelTools.name: clojure_symbol_info
languageModelTools.modelDescription: **Clojure Symbol Info** gets you detailed information about a Clojure symbol from the connected REPL. For ClojureDocs examples and see-alsos, use `clojuredocs_info` instead.
languageModelTools.userDescription: **Clojure Symbol Info** looks up documentation for a Clojure symbol.

clojureSymbol.description: The symbol to look up info for

namespace.description: Fully qualified namespace in which to evaluate the code. E.g. if calling functions in a file you are reading, it is probably the namespace of that file that should be provided.

replSessionKey.description: The Calva REPL session to evaluate the code in. Use the `list_sessions` tool to discover available sessions.

languageModelTools.name: clojuredocs_info
languageModelTools.modelDescription: **clojuredocs Lookup** looks up information, examples, see alsos, and more about a Clojure Core symbol from clojuredocs.org. For runtime symbol info from a connected REPL, use `clojure_symbol_info` instead.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get examples and usage from clojuredocs.org

clojureSymbol.description: The symbol to look up on clojuredocs.org

languageModelTools.name: clojure_repl_output_log
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
**Calva REPL Output** queries the REPL output log backed by Datascript. Each message is an entity with attributes: `:output/line` (monotonic integer), `:output/category` (`"evaluationResults"`, `"evaluatedCode"`, `"evaluationOutput"`, `"evaluationErrorOutput"`, `"otherOutput"`, `"otherErrorOutput"`), `:output/text`, `:output/who`, `:output/timestamp` (epoch ms), `:output/ns`, `:output/repl-session-key`, `:output/shadow-build`, `:output/shadow-runtime-id` (shadow-cljs only, when applicable).

`query`: a Datalog query as EDN string. `inputs` (optional): a JSON array of values for `:in` clause parameters (after `$`, which is supplied automatically). Use `(pull ?e [*])` for all attributes, or `(pull ?e [:output/line :output/text])` for specific ones.

All messages since line 42: `query: [:find [(pull ?e [*]) ...] :in $ ?since :where [?e :output/line ?l] [(> ?l ?since)]]`, `inputs: [42]`

Always load the `backseat-driver` skill when using this tool — it has more query examples and usage guidance.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Query Calva's REPL output log with Datalog

query.description: A Datalog query as an EDN string to run against the REPL output log. The database is passed automatically as the first `:in` argument. Prefer `pull` expressions to select only needed attributes.

inputs.description: A JSON array of values for parameterized queries. These are passed as additional `:in` arguments after `$`. Example: for `:in $ ?who ?since`, pass `["copilot", 42]`.

maxImages.description: Maximum number of images to return from image data found in the result. Defaults to 0 (no images). Pass a positive number to include images. Images beyond this cap are replaced with <<image-N-capped>> markers in the text but not returned as image content.

languageModelTools.name: clojure_balance_brackets
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
The **Bracket Balancer** AUTHORITATIVELY balances brackets in Clojure code. Always follow these steps when using it:

1. Pass the COMPLETE text you want to balance without any modifications
2. Use the EXACT output from this tool to replace the ENTIRE text you provided.

Always load the `editing-clojure-files` skill when using this tool.

text.description: The COMPLETE text of the file needing bracket balancing. Do not modify this text in any way before passing it to the tool.

languageModelTools.name: clojure_edit_files
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
**Edit Clojure Files** — perform structural editing across one or more Clojure files. Supports replace, insert, append, and create operations. Supports multiple edits across one or more files in a single call. Returns per-file diagnostics.

Always load the `editing-clojure-files` skill when using this tool.

Edit types:
- **replace**: Replace an existing top-level form identified by line number and target text
- **insert**: Insert a new top-level form at a position identified by line number and target text
- **append**: Append a top-level form at the end of a file
- **create**: Create a new file with content

Constraints:
- At most one `create` per file
- At most one `append` per file

edits.description: Array of edit operations to apply. Each edit specifies a file and operation type.

filePath.description: Absolute path to the target file

line.description: 1-based line number for targeting (required for replace/insert)

targetLineText.description: Text content of the target line for verification (required for replace/insert)

newForm.description: The new form content (required for replace/insert)

languageModelTools.name: clojure_load_file
languageModelTools.modelDescription: **Load Clojure File** — loads and evaluates an entire Clojure file. Returns the string result of the last evaluated form.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Load and evaluate a Clojure file in the connected REPL.

filePath.description: The absolute or workspace-relative path to the Clojure file to load and evaluate

replSessionKey.description: The REPL session to load the file in (e.g. 'clj', 'cljs'). Without this, Calva uses its default session routing.

who.description: A short, stable kebab-case slug identifying you as an evaluator. Think about what the session is about and what your role is in it, then sluggify that role and use it for `who`. Reuse the same slug across all evaluations in a session.

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run clean && npm run release
